remaining_tasks.note: FAILED at 2026-07-19T01:46:51Z, States.ExceedToleratedFailureThreshold: 1 failed / 4 aborted / 120 pending / 0 succeeded of 125. Root cause: fundamentals_ingest.py:213 hard-raise on parse_item_502() returning unresolved for required candidate 0000950170-24-098502 (item_502.py:519, ambiguous_verb_seen). No sanctioned bypass exists -- repair_manifest only gates force re-fetch, not this raise.

remaining_tasks.name: oprd bare-role + promoted-to-role fix (PR #187, d39b6c6)
remaining_tasks.note: Two purely-additive fixes (can only turn unresolved->applicable, never fabricate): bare object-predicate role with no preposition, and 'promoted to [Role]' scoped to the promote lemma. PARSER_VERSION 4->5. Re-measured against the 211 known-unresolved candidates: 22/211 (10.4%) now resolve, 189 still don't -- projects aggregate unresolved from ~10.6% to ~9.5%. Real but modest, NOT the dominant fix (earlier claim that oprd was 'clearly dominant' was based on eyeballing ~15 examples, not measured against the full set).

remaining_tasks.name: Strategic fork on remaining ~90% of unresolved population
remaining_tasks.note: Remaining patterns (backward-references to prior filings, bio-background prose, appositive names, nominalized 'approved the appointment of X') are suppression-shaped fixes -- CRITICAL SAFETY ASYMMETRY: fundamentals_ingest.py:213 raises on unresolved but does NOT re-check not_applicable, so a suppression fix that marks a false-trigger not_applicable can silently pass the gate while dropping a REAL event if the same filing also discloses one under a different construction. Confirmed on a real accession (0001104659-25-124213, CIK 1604191, GridAI Technologies) which has both a backward-reference AND a real new event nominalized under 'approved' rather than 'appointed'. Options presented to user: (a) keep patching remaining patterns (diminishing returns per the 10.4% result above), (b) route residual ~570-750 projected unresolved to a curation/review queue, (c) revisit whether zero-unresolved is the right bar for the strict gate. Not yet decided.

No-change rerun: zero SEC network, identical semantic digests
Final GO disposition
remaining_tasks.note: Separate Release Owner action (signed evidence tag) -- agent must report PASS evidence, not self-declare GO

blockers.name: Item 5.02 parser cannot resolve ~10.6% of required candidates (6+ distinct patterns, bullet-list sub-pattern already fixed)
blockers.detail: edgar_warehouse/parsers/item_502.py:519 returns Item502Result('unresolved', 'unclassified_named_event', ()) when an appointment/departure verb is found in a non-boilerplate sentence but no structured event can be extracted. fundamentals_ingest.py:213 hard-raises on this under release_mode. Refined 2,000-sample scope check: 10.6% unresolved, ~640-834 candidates projected across the 6,952-candidate freeze, spread across appoint (~5.2%: backward-references + appositive names), join (~3.55%: bio-background prose), terminate (~3.1%: conditional agreement boilerplate), plus smaller name/promote/elect/resign patterns. No repair/override path exists today; per-accession manual override is not viable at this scale for the remaining patterns either.

context_notes: Grok handed Ticket 20 GO prep to Claude. Claude completed all prep, obtained attestations, and fired the strict production execution with explicit user go-ahead. Execution ran ~50 min then failed on a genuine data-quality gate (unresolved Item 5.02 candidate), not an infra issue. Landed two safe parser fixes (bullet-list PR #185, oprd/promote PR #187) but real measurement against the known-unresolved population shows only a ~1 point aggregate improvement (10.6% -> ~9.5%), far short of what's needed. The remaining patterns carry real false-positive risk if fixed carelessly (confirmed via a real accession with both a backward-reference and a genuine new event under a different construction) -- surfaced the strategic fork to the user rather than continuing to patch unilaterally.
